items.question: A binary node has 30 class-0 and 10 class-1 samples. What is the Gini impurity?
A pure leaf (all one class) has entropy equal to:
items.explanation: No uncertainty when one class has p = 1 — H = −1·log₂(1) = 0.

items.question: CART selects a split by:
items.explanation: Greedy local criterion: best impurity reduction at the current node — not global accuracy optimization.

items.question: A regression tree leaf predicts:
options: Mean of y in the leaf
items.answer: Mean of y in the leaf
items.explanation: Regression CART minimizes variance — leaf value is the average target of training samples reaching that leaf.

items.question: Which hyperparameter most directly limits tree depth?
items.explanation: max_depth caps longest path from root to leaf. min_samples_leaf limits leaf size but does not directly cap depth alone.

items.question: Decision trees on numeric features typically require:
items.explanation: Axis-aligned threshold splits are invariant to monotonic rescaling per feature — scaling does not change partition structure.
